item.title: New York’s Tax on NYC Cash Home Purchases Is Close to Collapse
item.description: A proposal to levy a new tax on all-cash real estate transactions over $1 million in New York City is likely to be dropped from the New York state budget, according to people familiar with the negotiations.

item.title: Argentina’s Surge in Foreign Reserves Risks Reigniting Inflation
item.description: There are, by all appearances, more than enough dollars flowing into Argentina for the country to fulfill its pledge to replenish its depleted foreign reserves. Each week, they pour in, the result of factors such as a boom in commodity exports and corporate bond sales.

item.title: Turkey’s FX Sales Reach $6 Billion After Opposition Ruling
item.description: Turkish state lenders sold about $6 billion to defend the lira on Thursday, about half shortly after a court decision that removed the main opposition party’s leadership, according to traders familiar with the transactions.

item.title: StraightPath Founders Get Prison for $386 Million Pre-IPO Fraud
item.description: The founders of StraightPath Venture Partners LLC were sentenced to years in prison for defrauding investors who gave them $386 million to supposedly invest cheaply in hot companies about to go public.

item.title: Fed’s Barkin Says Repeated Supply Shocks Test Inflation Anchor
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said the ability of businesses and consumers to tolerate the latest in a series of supply shocks will determine whether the US central bank can continue to “look through” higher inflation without raising interest rates.

item.title: Billionaire Novogratz Fights $100 Million Fee on Dud Crypto Deal
item.description: Michael Novogratz, the billionaire founder of Galaxy Digital Inc., squared off in court this week with BitGo Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Mike Belshe in their protracted legal fight over a proposed merger that failed four years ago when the cryptocurrency markets cratered.

item.title: Oura, Maker of Popular Smart Rings, Files Confidentially for IPO
item.description: Oura Health Oy, a maker of popular smart rings that track health, fitness and sleep, filed confidentially for a US initial public offering, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Big Tech’s AI Debt Binge Tests High-Grade Market, Barclays Says
item.description: The biggest tech companies are borrowing more than expected to fund their spending on data centers and other artificial intelligence infrastructure, and the investment-grade bond market won’t be able to accommodate all of their financing needs.

item.title: UN Draft Treaty Aims to Boost Nations’ Rights to Tax Tech Giants
item.description: Countries at the United Nations are rewriting international tax rules in an effort to be able to tax technology giants like Alphabet and Amazon based on where their users are located rather than where they’re headquartered.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Hit Highest Since August as War Fans Inflation
item.description: US mortgage rates surged this week to the highest level since August, threatening a spring selling season that was just starting to get a pulse.

item.title: NYC Private School Bids $20 Million for Bankrupt Peer’s Campus
item.description: Nearly a year after Manhattan Country School went bankrupt and closed, its former campus has found a potential buyer in an up-and-coming school that offers what it refers to as a Classical Christian education.

item.title: France Will Add €710 Million in Aid to Offset High Energy Costs
item.description: French Budget Minister David Amiel pledged €710 million ($823 million) in new measures to support households and companies hit by rising energy costs due to the Iran war.

item.title: SpaceX Challenges AI Rivals for Control of $26.5 Trillion Market
item.description: SpaceX is marketing itself to IPO investors as an artificial intelligence play targeting a $26.5 trillion potential market opportunity, positioning itself to wrest business from investor darlings whose valuations have soared.

item.title: For Bitcoin ETF Holders, a Market Recovery Is a Reason to Sell
item.description: Bitcoin’s bad stretch of ETF outflows has exposed an uncomfortable dynamic in the crypto market’s structure: the price level that should draw buyers back in is the one that also prompts some of the heaviest selling.

item.title: BOE’s Bailey Says AI Will Take Time to Feed Through to Growth
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said productivity growth may slow down as companies and workers experiment with artificial intelligence, warning that the benefits could take time to feed through to the economy.

item.title: Spotify’s Concert Ticket Scarcity Solution: Save Top Fans Two Seats
item.description: The company will soon begin saving tickets for artists’ top fans in coordination with Live Nation.

item.title: Nuclear Company Deep Fission Files for $156 Million IPO
item.description: Deep Fission Inc. is seeking to raise up to $156 million in a US initial public offering, the latest nuclear startup to go that route as artificial intelligence data centers stoke demand for electricity.

item.title: Dimming Risk Appetites Welcome Development for US Equity Bulls
item.description: Beneath the surface of a stock market sitting at record highs, signs of trepidation are building.

item.title: Jet Fuel’s War Surge Vindicates Airlines Who Stuck With Hedging
item.description: The surge in the cost of jet fuel since the start of the Iran war is highlighting the vast disparity in how well individual airlines protect themselves against a price spike.

item.title: Why Staying Long Makes Sense With ETFs
item.description: Explore the genius of boring index funds and why being patient can reward you in long-term exchange-traded fund investing.

item.title: BOE’s Taylor Says Rate Rises Only Needed in Worst-Case Scenario
item.description: Only the most pessimistic Iran scenario clearly justifies raising interest rates, according to Bank of England policymaker Alan Taylor, who warned that the UK faces a higher recession risk because of the conflict.

item.title: Iran’s Ambassador to France Discusses Hormuz and Gulf Relations
item.description: Iranian Ambassador Mohammad Amin-Nejad sat down in Paris with Bloomberg News for an hour-long interview at his official residence.

item.title: How Bond Yield Surge Will Impact Economies, Markets
item.description: A prolonged period of elevated long-term bond yields is ramping up borrowing costs around the world. That’s because investors are demanding more and more compensation for holding government debt after the war in Iran ignited fresh inflation fears and concerns also swirl about the level of public debt and central bank interest rates.

item.title: Airbus, Air France Culpable for AF447 Crash, French Court Rules
item.description: Airbus SE and Air France were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter in the deadly crash of a passenger jet en route from Brazil to Paris 17 years ago after a French appeals court overturned a previous ruling.

item.title: Texans Hate Data Centers So Much They Are Asking Jesus for Help
item.description: In a state known for pro-business policies, some local activists are trying to block the big-money projects that power AI

item.title: AI Hardware Startup Hark Valued at $6 Billion in New Funding Round
item.description: Entrepreneur Brett Adcock’s new startup Hark has secured over $700 million in a funding round that values the artificial intelligence-focused hardware company at $6 billion, including the money raised.

item.title: Microsoft Turns to Video-Game Researcher to Fire Up Xbox Revival
item.description: Microsoft Corp. hired a longtime video-game industry analyst to help turn around its Xbox division.

item.title: US Manufacturing Expands Most in Four Years on Stockpiling Rush
item.description: US manufacturing activity expanded in May by the most in four years as customers strived to get ahead of mounting price pressures tied to the Iran war.

item.title: Russia Unveils Yuan Bond Sale Plan After Putin Visit to China
item.description: Russia announced plans to sell government bonds denominated in Chinese yuan for the second time after President Vladimir Putin returned from a two-day visit to China.

item.title: Citi’s Manthey Sees Europe Stock Gains Thanks to Earnings Growth
item.description: European stock markets could rise another 5% by the end of the year, driven by “an explosion of earnings,” said Beata Manthey, head of European equity strategy at Citigroup Global Markets Ltd.
